questions.q: Which constitutional office did G.V. Mavalankar hold first in independent India's Parliament?
options: First Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Ganesh Vasudev Mavalankar was the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: What does the 'G.V.' in G.V. Mavalankar's name stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Ganesh Vasudev Mavalankar.

questions.q: By what honorific title is G.V. Mavalankar often remembered?
questions.explanation: He is often called the 'Father of the Lok Sabha' for shaping its early procedures.

questions.q: In which year did G.V. Mavalankar become the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: He became the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha in 1952, after the first general election.

questions.q: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha is the presiding officer of which body?
questions.explanation: The Speaker presides over and conducts the business of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: G.V. Mavalankar served as the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha until which year?
questions.explanation: He served as Speaker from 1952 until 1956.

questions.q: The Lok Sabha, over which Mavalankar presided as Speaker, is also known as the:
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha is the House of the People, the lower house of Parliament.

questions.q: Which body did Mavalankar preside over immediately before the first Lok Sabha was constituted?
questions.explanation: He presided over the Constituent Assembly (Legislative) and the Provisional Parliament before the first Lok Sabha.

questions.q: From which present-day Indian state did G.V. Mavalankar primarily hail in his public life?
questions.explanation: Mavalankar was closely associated with Gujarat, especially Ahmedabad, in his political and legal career.

questions.q: What was Mavalankar's profession before he entered legislative office?
questions.explanation: Mavalankar trained and practised as a lawyer before his legislative career.

questions.q: Which event ended G.V. Mavalankar's tenure as the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha in 1956?
questions.explanation: Mavalankar died in office in 1956 while serving as Speaker.

questions.q: Mavalankar served as President of the Central Legislative Assembly before independence. What does that role correspond to today?
questions.explanation: The President of the Central Legislative Assembly was its presiding officer, a precursor to the Speaker's role.

questions.q: Under which constitutional article is the office of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha provided?
questions.explanation: Article 93 provides for the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Who elects the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, the office Mavalankar first held?
options: The members of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Speaker is elected by the members of the Lok Sabha from among themselves.

questions.q: In a tied vote in the Lok Sabha, what power does the Speaker exercise?
options: A double vote in every division
questions.explanation: The Speaker does not vote in the first instance but exercises a casting vote in the event of a tie.

questions.q: The first general election that led to Mavalankar's election as Speaker was held over which period?
questions.explanation: India's first general election was held from late 1951 to early 1952, and the first Lok Sabha then chose its Speaker.

questions.q: Which of these duties falls within the role of the Speaker that Mavalankar shaped?
options: Maintaining order and conducting House business
questions.explanation: The Speaker conducts the business of the House and maintains order during its proceedings.

questions.q: Mavalankar is credited with establishing conventions in which area of parliamentary functioning?
options: Procedures and rules of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: He is remembered for shaping the early procedures and conventions of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: To whom does the Speaker of the Lok Sabha tender resignation, as established for the office Mavalankar held?
questions.explanation: The Speaker may resign by writing to the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The Speaker's decision on whether a bill is a Money Bill is final. Mavalankar held this office; under which article is this certification provided?
questions.explanation: Article 110 deals with Money Bills, and the Speaker's certification on them is final.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Mavalankar particularly active as a public figure and lawyer?
questions.explanation: Mavalankar was prominently associated with Ahmedabad in his legal and civic life.

questions.q: Mavalankar's term as the first Lok Sabha Speaker fell within the term of which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: He was the Speaker of the first Lok Sabha, constituted after the 1951-52 election.

questions.q: Who succeeded G.V. Mavalankar as Speaker of the Lok Sabha after 1956?
questions.explanation: M. Ananthasayanam Ayyangar succeeded Mavalankar as Speaker after his death in 1956.

questions.q: Before the first Lok Sabha, Mavalankar served as Speaker of which body that functioned as a provisional Parliament?
questions.explanation: He served as Speaker of the Constituent Assembly (Legislative), which doubled as the Provisional Parliament.

questions.q: Mavalankar's leadership is associated with the formation of which forum of presiding officers?
options: The traditions of the Conference of Presiding Officers
questions.explanation: Mavalankar is associated with strengthening the institutional traditions of presiding officers in India.

questions.q: The 'Mavalankar Rule', a parliamentary convention named after him, relates to which matter?
options: The minimum support required to admit certain motions
questions.explanation: The convention associated with his name relates to the support a motion must command to be taken up in the House.

questions.q: Mavalankar took the chair of the first Lok Sabha as Speaker in which year, the same year the Lok Sabha first met?
questions.explanation: The first Lok Sabha met in 1952 and elected Mavalankar as its Speaker.

questions.q: Which earlier nationwide presiding role did Mavalankar hold before independence, in the central legislature of British India?
options: President of the Central Legislative Assembly
questions.explanation: He served as President of the Central Legislative Assembly before independence.

questions.q: The Speaker's office that Mavalankar pioneered is constitutionally expected to maintain which quality once a member is elected to it?
options: Strict impartiality in conducting the House
questions.explanation: The Speaker is expected to act with impartiality while presiding over the House.

questions.q: Mavalankar's tenure helped establish that the Speaker, once elected, continues in office until what point under normal circumstances?
options: Until immediately after a new Lok Sabha's first meeting
Until the next President is elected
questions.explanation: A Speaker normally continues in office until just before the first meeting of the newly elected Lok Sabha.
